content:
Hi everyone! :wave:

I'm Krisna, and I just submitted my first Linux kernel contribution!

What I Built:

A Rust implementation of the CLPS711X PWM driver as an alternative to the existing C driver (drivers/pwm/pwm-clps711x.c).

Status:

Submitted to LKML (linux-pwm and rust-for-linux mailing lists)

  • Patch: [PATCH] pwm: clps711x: Add Rust implementation

Build verified with LLVM=1 ARCH=arm64
Checkpatch passes (0 errors)
Maintains 100% functional parity with C version

Quick Details:

  • Hardware: Cirrus Logic CLPS711X PWM controller
  • 2 PWM channels, 4-bit duty cycle resolution
  • Device tree: cirrus,ep7209-pwm

My Questions

  1. Should I also open a PR to Rust-for-Linux/linux?
  2. Any specific guidelines I should follow?
  3. What's the typical review process?

Code

My branch: https://github.com/krishpranav/linux/tree/pwm-clps711x-rust

This is my first kernel contribution - I'd love feedback and guidance!

Thanks :)

Here are some sources that would help you:

  • https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submit-checklist-addendum
  • https://docs.kernel.org/rust/coding-guidelines.html
  • https://docs.kernel.org/process/submitting-patches.html

The typical review process on the kernel is that you find the maintainers for the subtree you are changing. You send your patch to them and the public mailing list. You can get all the addresses by using the get_maintainer.pl script. The response time depends on the current workload of the maintainers, but usually you start to get some feedbacks in a couple of days.
Thank you too for putting an effort on the kernel!
@Onur Özkan are you one of the maintainer?
Okay cool, I have submitted a patch to the official Linux kernel repository. Would it also be appropriate to submit a corresponding patch to the Rust-for-Linux repository?

Krisna Pranav said:

Okay cool, I have submitted a patch to the official Linux kernel repository. Would it also be appropriate to submit a corresponding patch to the Rust-for-Linux repository?

I didn't get what you mean here. You don't submit patches to repository; you send them to the mailing lists (basically run get_maintainer script for the files you changed and send your patches to all those addresses)
and your patches shouldn't really be different rather they are based on torvalds/linux or rust-for-linux/linux, they are almost identical today.
yup, i already sent to those maintainers. thanks for it

Welcome!

In the link @Onur Özkan wrote, I would suggest also reading the https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#the-kernel-development-process section (which links to the other documents and explains a bit the kernel development process and how to approach it for Rust). In general, I suggest reading that "Contributing" page and related pages -- they should answer most of the common questions.

as an alternative to the existing C driver

Usually maintainers do not accept duplicate implementations. But please see the rest of "Contributing" as well as https://rust-for-linux.com/rust-reference-drivers in case it helps.

Submitted to LKML (linux-pwm and rust-for-linux mailing lists)

Hmm... Did it arrive there? Do you see it in lore.kernel.org?

I hope that helps!
did you configure your email client correctly? https://docs.kernel.org/process/email-clients.html
It may be that it contained HTML. That gets emails dropped.
